item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a great entry.

My only question is why Recovered File 2090/44 states that basketball should be promoted from the "beginning of the early 19th century on". James Naismith invented basketball in 1891, so surely this sentence should read "from the late 19th century on", unless it is either meant to imply that basketball began many decades earlier in the Foundation-verse, or that intervention to ensure basketball's creation must begin many decades in advance.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hmm…basketball is foundation-made…
That's interesting. +1

I want to ask a question. This article has "euclid" tag. But SCP-2090 is Neutralized.
I think "neutralized" tag is suitable.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The lead-up into "basketball games are a containment ritual" was very well-done, and I didn't expect it would go there. When it did, I was in stitches.

Giddy +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is so fucking stupid. I LOVE IT. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes.Just yes

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This feels like it should be a -J, but, dammit, it's just so well written…

edit: though, just realised after backtracking a page in the comments… at first, we have a note about how it would be unfeasible to remove Tim from the public sphere due to the fame and popularity he's built up… but after that, we get a whole line of worldbuilding that reveals that he's only in that position of fame and noteriety because the foundation put him there in the first place. Whilst the two threads aren't necessarily incompatible, the way they're presented makes the latter seem to contradict the former in terms of apparent causality?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm trying to think of what the DMN link would be, but all I can think of is the "he's on fire!" commentary from the old NBA Jam games crossed with old father McNinja's sage advice about how to avoid capture by other ninjas…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I expected a more explicit connection to the ritual ball game of the Mayans and related cultures in the article, and more Dr. McNinja references in the comment section. Probably for the best to have left out both of those things, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked the ending better before this update, but I recognize that there's really no other choice, and it still ends quite nicely. +1

item.title: Re: QUESTIONS
item.content:encoded: See my comment a page back

item.title: QUESTIONS
item.content:encoded: What happens now, since he retired? Lol

item.title: Basketball Twist
item.content:encoded: I might be in the minority here, but I think the end twist that basketball was devised to keep him check - while absurd - is fantastic and creative. The article builds up to it well, building suspense with an intriguing and surprising payoff. Upvote, great job.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: A casual search shows that SCP-3030 also uses site-56. Maybe it's not a bad idea to add an inline link to make it symmetrical.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

Got a chance to talk to kaktus about this one, and we both agreed that it is one article that falls more on the self-contained side of things with respect to the Wikiwalk. Still, there is a mention of Site-45, which means a link there from here would allow someone to start at the Site-81 dossier, go to SCP-715 which links here, which would then link to Site-45, opening up a wide variety of articles to the reader. As such, I am recommending the following edit:

Changing this:

The GODHEAD has moved through Site-45 and Site-56. Full scale containment breaches are active, likely the western seaboard is collapsed by now.

To this:

The GODHEAD has moved through Site-45 and Site-56. Full scale containment breaches are active, likely the western seaboard is collapsed by now.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Here's one where not only a bunch of celebrities were made SCPs, it ended killing them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …holy shit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: O5s are already on the watch

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I would point out that while Tim Duncan has retired, Protocol Ōllamaliztli-5 continues to remain effective- sports nerds have determined that Steph Curry is mathematically impossible. X3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just noticed something in Recovered File 2090/1, the number 2016. Does this mean djkaktus correctly predicted the year Tim Duncan retires?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I actually have a solid idea for writing a post retirement canon tale on this, one that might even lead to an addendum change and new classification for this scp but I cant seem to get a hold of Kaktus :/

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Someone better make sure he becomes a general manager or something… or we're headed for an XK real quick.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It is unlikely that he will stop participating in the ritual completely; who does?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Tim Duncan's retiring tonight. What happens if SCP-2090 retires?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So…basketball is a mystical ritual designed to stop Tim Duncan from destroying the world?

Just weird and mysterious enough for a +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In regards to the classification, I think the reason it's Euclid is because A) he doesn't seem to be overtly hostile, and B) he's well-contained as long as he keeps performing the ritual.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was never good at basketball (actually, sports as a general), so good luck containing this thing, Foundation.

Seriously, making real person or history into SCP is a one bold move and should be applauded for even trying, but simultaneously it should be open to criticism about absurdity regarding the difference between the article and reality - like, making containment procedure before Tim Duncan was born because he is going to Godhead in the future? and we only recently found out he shows anomalous sign from Kant Reader? What timeline is this?

EDIT: OK got it, first found out Tim Duncan is anomalous, got a warning message from future (or alternate timeline, whatever), so created basketball and sent the protocol to early 19th century..
or was the Future Foundation the one who sent the protocol to 19th century?
Something's still missing here, but I'll stop for the moment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: " Due to the high level of celebrity that SCP-2090 has obtained, it was considered unfeasible to remove the subject from the public and place it within containment for further observation." this doesnt make sense, celebrities get in accidents and fall out of popularity all the time, but other than that this is pretty awesome.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I find it really interesting that kaktus writes about the Foundation creating an extremely popular cereal as containment and nobody bats an eye, but when the Foundation popularizes basketball (people keep saying they created it, but file 2090/43 implies it's already there in some form) people call that going too far.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I respect how ambitious you were being here, but I just find this concept so absurd, to the point where it makes me go "yeah, not really." I'm willing to accept the Foundation having to do/having the power to do lots of stuff, but inventing an entire sport to contain an SCP is not one of them. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Kaktus you mad fucking genius.

Everybody who downvoted this is gonna get dunked on when the apocalypse comes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I know this is a bit of a necro and it's the title of an Onion article but:

"Groans Abound As Tim Duncan Raises Hand Once Again At City Council Meeting"

There's no way this isn't the potential title of a tale for this skip and Cross City City City City Hall.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I enjoy all the commentary on these pages trying to justify why this SCP doesn't make logical sense, even though it does, and the reality is it's just too far-fetched and silly for most people. Which is fine. But we live in an era of trying to make taste preferences objective assessments that everyone else must share.

I mean, don't get me wrong, this is a terrible SCP. But also great. But also terrible.

I'm upvoting it pretty hard, but I'm headcanoning it as definite misinformation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this. I've been reading SCP for a good year and this isthe first article to make me comment. This is what SCP is about for me. The link to real world events. An explanation for a commonly accepted phenomenon. To make it conspiratory. When I opened the last document to reveal that it is basketball, my mouth dropped. Thank you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a pretty easy question. There was a week in chat a while back where Kate McTiriss couldn't get off of the idea of Tim Duncan as a skip. I finally offered to write it if she'd stop talking about it, and this was the result.

All things considered, it turned out pretty well.

Wait, is Steve Nash still in the league?

…

Christ, Steve Nash is still in the league. That dude is not young.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Why Tim Duncan? This isn't a criticism, more I'm curious what led to his selection specifically. Why not, say, Lebron? Or Steve Nash?

This article is also even funnier in light of the series of Onion articles focused on Duncan. In those he is primarily described as painfully boring.

"Groans Abound As Tim Duncan Raises Hand Once Again At City Council Meeting"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ihpkmn, it would only qualify as Libel if it could be proven that djkaktus is maliciously trying to smear Tim Duncan's name, and if this could actually harm his reputation/public image. (There's a few other qualifiers, but those are the big'ins).

I don't think this does either of these.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This comment makes me wonder if you came down as hard on the image of a former president of the united states being cut into pieces, or the image relating to the article where several well known Miami Heat and Boston Celtics players are ritualistically sacrificed.

Or if it's just me.

Edit: Additionally, and I don't mean to say this like an asshole (although I fear I will), your comment makes me think that you don't know how libel works. If I were insisting this was truth, then there might be an argument (somewhere?), but as it stands, the wiki is a work of fiction, and I am a fiction author.

That being said, one last thing:

That's just plain stupid

Thanks, buddy. Shame you couldn't have just approached me with these concerns. I'll keep this in mind.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't mind the Kant counter stuff at all, unlike a lot of people on this thread.

What I do mind is that you're making a person who is currently alive, is a major figure in the world of sports, and probably has a team of very powerful lawyers, into an SCP that could end the world, and you're doing it without a hint of irony, and a complete disregard for the fact that the wiki could get its pants sued off for using Duncan's image, name, etc on here in a way that I'm almost sure qualifies as libel.

Also, the Foundation is responsible for Basketball. That's just plain stupid, and not in a good way either.

-1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the concept that basketball is essentially the containment procedures for someone. I can also buy how, without this, he would essentially go Berserker and kill everything or destroy reality.

What I can't buy, and what breaks my suspension of disbelief, is that the SCP Foundation could keep sending things back in time to different universes in order to keep hammering out the details on how to contain this SCP. It seems like a cop-out of sorts, even if it fits and makes sense in this. Time travel is generally an iffy thing with me, and here I just plain can't get into this.

I still think that the basketball containment is a great idea, but shouldn't this be Keter? What happens when he retires? Won't he go Godhead then? Does the Foundation then capture him and make him play basketball all the time? Why does it have to be professional basketball? I think the fact that the basketball containment is the twist, rather than something to hook people on, makes it a bit weaker. And this is just my personal thing, but Kant and Humes seems sort of silly to me. It's not inherently a bad thing, it just couldn't help me get into this.

All of this means a downvote from me, but hey; at +43, you're clearly doing something right.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the subtle implication that Tim Duncan might not actually be properly contained, given that the file containing the particulars for Ōllamaliztli-5 (specifically, how the rules and regulations should develop over time) was largely unreadable. The ritual is being performed, but do they know it's being performed correctly?

Also, that file contained a reference to "SCP-N/A". Was that intentional, given that it was from a previous iteration? Or is it a placeholder that wasn't replaced with the article number after posting?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That requires work. :|

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Against my better judgement, I'm upvoting this.

On second thought, downvote. I'm not against the idea of the Foundation being involved in the inception of actual major events/phenomena nor the idea of SCPs involving actual people, but this doesn't quite cut it for me. I'm not a big fan of how the general public isn't aware of Tim Duncan's anomalous nature because it's conveniently only detectable by Foundation-grade tech, and the punch at the end is easily predicted if you considered a couple details given beforehand.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No worries. Friendly point of note, and forgive me if this is something you're already aware of, but if you check the history of the page at the bottom of it, the original author is whoever made the 0th edit :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Okay, since I can't edit my comment…

Having seen xamrin's and AgentMacLeod's comments below, I think I'm going to downvote this. I'm not comfortable with the idea of saying "this real, living person is actually an anomaly".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I of course don't think you are one. But given that I don't know the author until they identify themselves in the comments, it's very easy to assume that's what's happening.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Really, this is addressed in SCP-2000.

These explain the Hitler and containment breach problems pretty well I think:
Hitlers Time Travel Exemption Act
Butterfly Of Doom
Every change you make ripples outwards to change everything else- maybe a little, maybe a lot.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Meanwhile, in-universe, a foundation scientist is cursing because SCP-2317 has suddenly begun showing awareness of and resistance to protocol 2317-153$\Delta$7, a ritual designed to reinforce its remaining chain by representing via 3d object and 2d plane the repulsion of a 4d entity crossing the barrier between 3d worlds…
"We need more pigeons and cats! NOW!"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I would also like to know this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That ending note made this major-league cool for me.

Pun. So. Intended.

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You're thinking of SCP-1943, where Chaos Insurgency tried to capture her (but then again, how reliable are they?).

Anyways, I feel this is one of those "tales via SCP" articles, where the skip itself is completely mundane but the fun part is the Foundation's actions. Having the fact that it's basketball upfront would lead to some very awkward structuring if there was any interest in keeping it interesting.

I will agree however that the discovery could be more interesting than just "hey, aren't we lucky we had these tools scanning at a basketball game?".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: slightly relevant

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As long as he doesn't learn how to do a Chaos Dunk, we oughta be ok.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Actually, basketball seems to be a containment procedure…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
God Dammit. I saw a negative image of Tim Duncan and I knew I was going to downvote. Then, Basketball as a containment.

I do have to point out though, that is the old international court layout, not the NBA court.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: But it was the tape that was anomalous, not Reagan. This SCP is using the identity of a real (and famous) person.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not sure how I feel about being an implied bandwagon jumper, as if I need Kate's ideas to make my own succeed (not to say i don't need Kate but that's a different matter entirely). I feel as if my work thus far has been able to stand on its own.

It was just a nod to an idea i liked from an author I talked to specifically about this article. Nothing more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No, I mean, nuking the volcano to contain 076-2. Where did that idea come from? Is it in a Tale or something?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There now needs to be a Competitive Eschatology tale featuring Tim Duncan, Destroyer of Worlds.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: do you mean the volcano?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What's all this stuff about Mt. Saint Helens? Link?
